project.name: NestGenie
project.description: SMS-first AI family assistant. Parents text NestGenie; it responds, sends briefings, manages outbound messages to contacts. Web companion for onboarding, calendar linking, briefings history, and privacy controls.
project.web_role: thin companion — not the primary UX surface

tech_stack.web: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, TanStack Query, react-hook-form, zod
tech_stack.auth: SMS OTP → JWT in httpOnly cookie (no NextAuth, no passwords)
tech_stack.ai: AWS Bedrock — Claude Haiku (classify) → Claude Sonnet (reason) → Claude Opus (high-stakes)

layouts.name: (marketing) layout
layouts.description: Public unauthenticated pages. Centered single column max-w-md. Sticky header with NestGenie logo.

layouts.name: (app) layout
layouts.description: Authenticated parent experience. Desktop: sidebar nav (256px) + main content max-w-3xl. Mobile: topbar + sticky bottom nav (Home, Family, Briefings, Calendar, Settings). Middleware redirects unauthenticated to /sign-in.

screen_set.name: Landing / marketing home
screen_set.description: Public landing page explaining NestGenie. Hero section: 'AI that keeps your family running — just text it.' Explains SMS-first interaction model. Primary CTA: 'Sign in'. No sign-up — parents are added when an admin registers their phone number.
key_elements: SMS-first explanation section with phone number illustration
3 feature tiles: Daily briefings, Outbound assist, Parenting guidance
Footer: Privacy policy link, SMS terms link
screen_set.responsive_notes: Full-width hero on desktop (max-w-5xl), single column on mobile. No sidebar.

screen_set.name: Sign-in — phone entry
screen_set.description: Single-field form for phone number entry. Submits to request OTP. Validates E.164 format inline.
key_elements: PhoneInput component with US flag selector

screen_set.name: Sign-in — OTP verify
screen_set.description: 6-digit OTP entry. Shows masked phone number. Resend option (rate-limited). On success, redirects to /app or /app/onboarding.

screen_set.name: Dashboard shell
screen_set.description: Main landing page after sign-in. Shows NestGenie status (active phone number + channel health) and quick-link tiles. First-time users see onboarding prompt card.
key_elements: Status card: NestGenie phone number, last message timestamp, channel health chip (Active/Down)
5 quick-link tiles: Family, Briefings, Calendar, Outbound, Settings (each with icon + label + brief description)
First-time banner: 'Finish setting up your family profile' → /app/onboarding (shown if family not complete)
Empty state if no recent activity

screen_set.name: Settings shell
screen_set.description: Settings hub with navigation list to sub-pages. M0 is a shell with links; sub-pages are added in M2/M4.
key_elements: Settings nav list: Connection, Privacy & Data, Notifications, Parenting Philosophy
Each item: icon + label + description + chevron

screen_set.name: Connection status
screen_set.description: Shows NestGenie's SMS and email channel health. Active phone number, last inbound/outbound, delivery status.
key_elements: SMS channel card: active number, A2P status chip, last inbound timestamp
Email channel card: FROM address, last email delivered timestamp

screen_set.name: Onboarding — welcome
screen_set.description: Step 1 of onboarding wizard. Explains what NestGenie does, tells parent they can do this over SMS or here.
key_elements: Body: 'You can finish this here or text NestGenie at +1 555 555 5555 anytime.'

screen_set.name: Onboarding — parenting philosophy
screen_set.description: Multi-select chips for parenting philosophy. Saves to families.philosophy_hints.
key_elements: Heading: 'How do you like to parent?'
Subtext: 'This helps NestGenie match your style when giving guidance.'
Chip grid: Attachment, Montessori, Free-range, Positive discipline, Gentle parenting, Structured, Evidence-based, Intuitive, Other

screen_set.name: Onboarding — family profile
screen_set.description: Capture parent display name, children (expandable list), pediatrician contact, allergies.
key_elements: 'Add child' expandable section: name, DOB, allergies/conditions
'Add pediatrician' expandable: name, phone (used for medical referral drafts)

screen_set.name: Onboarding — consent
screen_set.description: Privacy consent gate. Cannot complete onboarding without checking. Covers MHMDA and medical disclaimer.
key_elements: Scrollable privacy summary (NOT full legal text — plain English ~200 words)
MHMDA note: 'NestGenie may process family-related information. It is not a mental health service.'
Medical disclaimer: 'NestGenie will always suggest you check with your pediatrician for medical questions.'

screen_set.name: Family — list
screen_set.description: Overview of household: children + contacts. Quick-add buttons.
key_elements: Children section: FamilyMemberCard per child (name, age, allergies badges)
Contacts section: FamilyMemberCard per contact (name, relationship, TCPA consent chip)

screen_set.name: Family — child profile
screen_set.description: Create or edit a child record. Name, DOB, allergies, school, routines, notes.
key_elements: ChildProfileForm: name, DOB picker, allergies (tag input), school, routines textarea, notes
Save + Delete (edit mode only, with confirmation dialog)
Age computed display: '4 years old (turns 5 in March)'

screen_set.name: Family — contact
screen_set.description: Create or edit a third-party contact. Captures TCPA consent state.
key_elements: Fields: name, relationship (select: grandparent, caregiver, daycare, coach, teacher, other), phone (E.164)
TCPA consent section: 'Has [name] agreed to receive messages from you via NestGenie?' Toggle. If yes: records consent timestamp.
ContactConsentFlow inline if tcpa_consent being set for first time

screen_set.name: Privacy / consent / data export
screen_set.description: Privacy controls: view consent, export data, delete account, notification preferences.
key_elements: Consent record card: consent given date, link to full privacy policy
Data export card: 'Export my data (JSON)' button → triggers async export, emails download link
Delete data card: 'Delete all my data' (destructive, confirmation dialog with 'Type DELETE to confirm')
Notification preferences: SMS vs email toggles per type (briefings, reminders, outbound confirmations)

screen_set.name: Briefings history
screen_set.description: Read-only list of last 30 days of briefings NestGenie sent. Filter by date.
key_elements: Date filter: today / this week / this month / all (30d)

screen_set.name: Briefing detail
screen_set.description: Full briefing text. Medical-flag overlay if applicable. Channel chip. Citations after M4.
key_elements: PageHeader: briefing date + channel chip (SMS / Email)
KB-grounded answer component (M4 addition): inline citations with source title + philosophy tag chip

screen_set.name: Calendar — connect
screen_set.description: Google Calendar OAuth connect/disconnect and sync status.
key_elements: CalendarConnectionCard: disconnected state → 'Connect Google Calendar' button (initiates OAuth)
Connected state: last sync timestamp, scopes granted, event count synced, 'Disconnect' CTA
Note: 'NestGenie reads your primary calendar to include events in your daily briefings.'

screen_set.name: Outbound audit log — list
screen_set.description: Read-only audit log of all outbound AI-drafted messages (to third-party contacts). Shows draft → approved → sent trail.
key_elements: Filter tabs: All / Pending / Sent / Declined
Pending items have 'Review & approve' CTA → opens ConfirmSendSheet

screen_set.name: Outbound audit log — detail
screen_set.description: Full trail for one outbound action. Draft → confirmed → sent chain with timestamps.
key_elements: Timeline: Queued → Draft generated (by NestGenie) → Parent confirmed → Sent

screen_set.name: Parenting philosophy selector
screen_set.description: Settings page to edit parenting philosophy chips post-onboarding.
key_elements: Same chip grid as onboarding step S08
Note: 'NestGenie uses this to match its guidance to your parenting style.'

screen_set.name: Notification preferences
screen_set.description: Toggle SMS vs email per notification type. No push in MVP.
key_elements: Section per type: Daily briefings, Reminders, Outbound confirmations
Each: SMS toggle + email toggle (at least one required)

agent_kickoff_message:
You are starting NestGenie — an SMS-first AI family assistant. The primary interaction is via SMS (Twilio); the web companion (Next.js 14) is a thin authenticated surface for onboarding, family profile, briefings history, outbound audit, calendar linking, and privacy controls.

Your tasks:
1. Scaffold the monorepo: `/web` (Next.js 14 App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui) and `/api` (FastAPI Python 3.12 async)
2. Set up the two layouts: `(marketing)` for public pages and `(app)` for authenticated pages with middleware auth guard
3. Build M0 screens first: Landing (/), Sign-in phone (/sign-in), Sign-in OTP (/sign-in/verify), Dashboard shell (/app), Settings shell (/app/settings)
4. Wire auth: POST /auth/request-otp → POST /auth/verify-otp → httpOnly JWT cookie (no NextAuth, no passwords)
5. Create Alembic migrations for families, parents, children, contacts, conversations, messages

Design rules: green brand (#2E7D32), shadcn/ui components, mobile-first (min 360px), empty states with SMS nudge, TanStack Query for all data fetching, react-hook-form + zod for all forms.

See replit.md for complete rules, design-system/guide.md for palette and layout, design-system/components.md for component inventory, and screen-specs/request.json for per-screen specs and API routes.

Do NOT build: native apps, push notifications, admin console, voice channels, Apple/Outlook calendar, dark mode, consumer billing (CB add-on is a separate sprint).
